That under the Packers and Stockyards Act and the famous decision of Stafford against Wallace of this Court immediately following the enactment of the Act, the Stock Yard Company is a public utility and being such is bound to furnish -- as in all other market agencies with stockyard services without discrimination.

turns.name: CHARLES E. WHITTAKER
turns.text: Now Mr. Thomas, may I ask you on that sir? Where are you to render the reasonable stockyard services at a particular registration authorized?

turns.name: HADLOND P. THOMAS
turns.text: No, are we to render or whereas the stockyard to render, it makes a difference.

turns.name: CHARLES E. WHITTAKER
turns.text: Well, to you. Were you --

turns.name: HADLOND P. THOMAS
turns.text: All right. As a (Voice Overlap) --

turns.name: CHARLES E. WHITTAKER
turns.text: -- registration to operate itself or not and the session stopped, you must furnish reasonable stockyard service without registration (Inaudible)

turns.name: HADLOND P. THOMAS
turns.text: We'd render them there in that instance of course.

turns.name: CHARLES E. WHITTAKER
turns.text: Now then under your permit (Inaudible)

turns.name: HADLOND P. THOMAS
turns.text: Right.

turns.name: CHARLES E. WHITTAKER
turns.text: Now then, do you have rights you think to divert business which normally would flow to Denver over the mountains to Salt Lake?

turns.name: HADLOND P. THOMAS
turns.text: I think so. We say so.

turns.name: CHARLES E. WHITTAKER
turns.text: And -- and does a regulation opposing that you think here valid.

turns.name: HADLOND P. THOMAS
turns.text: We do.

turns.name: CHARLES E. WHITTAKER
turns.text: In the vacuum and without any fact.

turns.name: HADLOND P. THOMAS
turns.text: We do, as violative of the fact that the stockyard cites which commands the Stock Yard Company to furnish us with stockyard services and to enact and observe nondiscriminatory regulations. We get back to the non -- to the discriminatory again. We say that's discriminatory. Their Court of Appeals said so too from up the cowboy country, if Your Honor please.

turns.name: CHARLES E. WHITTAKER
turns.text: Whom I --

turns.name: WILLIAM J. BRENNAN, JR.
turns.text: (Inaudible)

turns.name: HADLOND P. THOMAS
turns.text: Now, of course every public utility is bound to serve and we have cited the cases, many of them, which hold that a public utility may not demand exclusive patronage or exclusive allegiance for example an electric company cannot say if you -- if you burn gas also, you can have our electricity. And a railroad company can't say if you ride the airplanes, you can't ride our -- our trains and so on and so forth. Nor public utilities deny service because of something which is a collateral matter -- something which is collateral to the service they furnish. For example, I can't say to you that if you can have water and electricity but only if you install a septic tank. Now we say here that this regulation demands our exclusive patronage our exclusive allegiance and also it denies us service because of a collateral matter namely doing business out in the country. The country business is big if the Court please. That's one reason we're objecting and we say we've got a right to go out in the country and sell from one grower to another and that the Denver Stock Yard Company can't stop us by regulation.

turns.name: HUGO L. BLACK
turns.text: Would you mind -- would you mind explaining the technique of how you do your business.

turns.name: HADLOND P. THOMAS
turns.text: Well, we do two kinds of business, if the Court please. We do a business, a grower will give us -- send us livestock to sell on consignment for example at the stockyard and we will ship it there and it will be sold there, on the other hand -- some grower will say to us find a buyer for our livestock and we do --

turns.name: HUGO L. BLACK
turns.text: Do you travel around over the country?

turns.name: HADLOND P. THOMAS
turns.text: We have people out in the country. We do have tremendous country business, if the Court please. That's what it states here.

turns.name: HUGO L. BLACK
turns.text: Does -- does everything that you buy go to the stockyard?

turns.name: HADLOND P. THOMAS
turns.text: Oh, no, not near. No. I should say not. I would say most of it sold in the country.

turns.name: HUGO L. BLACK
turns.text: Are there some places where they're reasonably close to one -- more than one stockyard?

turns.name: HADLOND P. THOMAS
turns.text: It might be in the Midwest. I don't think there is in the west where Denver, where Los Angeles, Salt Lake, Ogden and then there are yards that are not posted for example, Phoenix and Brawley. They're not imposed to stockyard subject to jurisdiction of the -- of the (Voice Overlap) --

turns.name: HUGO L. BLACK
turns.text: But if it's not any along close to the dividing line, what is the quarrel about?

turns.name: HADLOND P. THOMAS
turns.text: Well, the quarrel is about whether we can sell -- buy and sell in the country part of Colorado or whether we've got to ship all that stuff to the Denver yard.

turns.name: WILLIAM O. DOUGLAS
turns.text: But the judicial officer didn't so construe it. He construed the controversy before him as one where Denver Stock Yard was compelled to furnish its facilities to the benefit of its competitors in Kansas City.

turns.name: HADLOND P. THOMAS
turns.text: Well --

turns.name: WILLIAM O. DOUGLAS
turns.text: As he addressed himself to that solicitation in the Stock Yard that I asked you about (Voice Overlap) --

turns.name: HADLOND P. THOMAS
turns.text: Well, we've -- there's no question about it or what. We will get livestock in the country and ship it to Kansas City if somebody wants us to. And right then, we'd be violating and we would report this regulation.

turns.name: HUGO L. BLACK
turns.text: When that's ordinarily done, is there a dispute over to crime?

turns.name: HADLOND P. THOMAS
turns.text: Well, I don't know why it's (Voice Overlap). It maybe -- it maybe that the grower will say we -- I think you can get me a better price at Kansas City or we may think of the case.

turns.name: CHARLES E. WHITTAKER
turns.text: Which should be violating this regulation if you bought cattle in the east or in the west -- Eastern 16 of Colorado and ship them on or encourage others to ship them on to Kansas City?

turns.name: HADLOND P. THOMAS
turns.text: No.

turns.name: CHARLES E. WHITTAKER
turns.text: You would not, would you?

turns.name: HADLOND P. THOMAS
turns.text: We would not.

turns.name: CHARLES E. WHITTAKER
turns.text: And why is that?

turns.name: HADLOND P. THOMAS
turns.text: Because they say that -- they take the position at least that this thing implies today only to that West 56.

turns.name: CHARLES E. WHITTAKER
turns.text: Yes.

turns.name: HADLOND P. THOMAS
turns.text: Now, if they were in this lawsuit, they're going to push those boundaries all over the United States like Los Angeles has done or has attempted to do.

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: The Secretary of Agriculture might have to say about that, wouldn't he?

turns.name: HADLOND P. THOMAS
turns.text: Well, he hasn't said anything yet and Los Angeles' regulation is passed the time of complaint.

turns.name: CHARLES E. WHITTAKER
turns.text: Now, you wouldn't have to be a market agency on the Denver Stock Yard in order to make your country transactions (Inaudible)?

turns.name: HADLOND P. THOMAS
turns.text: Oh, no. That would result if we have to.

turns.name: CHARLES E. WHITTAKER
turns.text: And -- and would this not be the means if all other market agencies on the Denver yards, transact their business outside the yards of destroying the public market Denver?

turns.name: HADLOND P. THOMAS
turns.text: Which they do, if the Court please. They do just like we do.

turns.name: CHARLES E. WHITTAKER
turns.text: Wouldn't this as a matter of fact if you all did what you're doing, (Inaudible) to destroy the public market in Denver?

turns.name: HADLOND P. THOMAS
turns.text: I -- I supposed that if you -- you ask me if we and all the other market agencies, buy and sell in the country, it reduces the livestock that moves for the Denver Stock Yard of course and that's why of the regulation. Now, let me say this too, that the American Stockyard Association in their brief amicus curiae has pointed out that very thing that when the Packers and Stockyards Act was passed in 1921, 90% of all livestock moved through these posted stockyards. Whereas today, only about 60% moves through and it's going down all the time, you can see the reason for the regulation, trying to force this economic effect.

turns.name: CHARLES E. WHITTAKER
turns.text: You do not think in that circumstance, Mr. Thomas that would it be essential to have a factual showing in order to determine whether or not there were evils needed to be corrected by this resolution or by these regulations that whether they were responsive?

turns.name: HADLOND P. THOMAS
turns.text: No, I do not.

turns.name: CHARLES E. WHITTAKER
turns.text: You do not.

turns.name: HADLOND P. THOMAS
turns.text: I think it would be -- they are trying to get the law changed if they can. Well I say this -- I beg your pardon.

turns.name: EARL WARREN
turns.text: Under -- under this law, is the Secretary of Agriculture be permitted to sort of walk the lines with the cattle country -- states to and say that one stockyard (Inaudible) necessary other than -- other than another one?

turns.name: HADLOND P. THOMAS
turns.text: I don t think he has any such power, Mr. Chief Justice.

turns.name: EARL WARREN
turns.text: (Inaudible) power.

turns.name: HADLOND P. THOMAS
turns.text: I never heard of that. Now, I don't think that I have anymore to say Your Honors. I think you have the case very well in mind. It -- it went off entirely on the question of the illegality of this thing upon its face as a matter of law and we say that is exactly where we stood and where we continue to stand. If we are right and the Court of Appeals is right, the judgment must be affirmed. Thank you. If I have ten more minutes --- moment --

turns.name: EARL WARREN
turns.text: I beg your pardon.

turns.name: HADLOND P. THOMAS
turns.text: I understood I have time for rebuttal.

turns.name: EARL WARREN
turns.text: Well, I thought the red light was on for sometime before you sat down.

turns.name: HADLOND P. THOMAS
turns.text: Well, I am answering Mr. Justice Black's question as I understood.

turns.name: EARL WARREN
turns.text: No, I think your time is up. I think your time is up and it's up for sometime before you sat down. The red light -- I beg your pardon. Yes.

turns.name: HUGO L. BLACK
turns.text: I asked him a question after the time (Voice Overlap) --

turns.name: HADLOND P. THOMAS
turns.text: I'm sorry. I --

turns.name: EARL WARREN
turns.text: I think it's just in our side. That's all we have in this case.
